title: rectpuls

gpu_support.notes: GPU inputs are gathered to the host and evaluated with the scalar reference implementation.

fusion.notes: Fusion is disabled because width validation and boundary half-amplitude semantics are handled by the host builtin.

description: `rectpuls(T)` returns samples of a unit-width rectangular pulse centered at zero. `rectpuls(T, W)` uses pulse width `W`.
behaviors: Operates element-wise on real numeric scalars, vectors, matrices, and N-D tensors; preserves input shape.
Returns `1` for `abs(T) < W/2`, `0.5` at `abs(T) == W/2`, and `0` outside the pulse.
The width must be positive and finite.
Integer and logical inputs are promoted to double precision before evaluation.
Complex and text inputs are rejected with a builtin-scoped error.

faqs.question: What happens at the exact pulse edge?
faqs.answer: RunMat returns `0.5` at `abs(T) == W/2`, matching the standard sampled rectangular pulse convention.

faqs.question: Can `rectpuls` be used inside `pulstran`?
faqs.answer: Yes. `pulstran(T, D, 'rectpuls', W)` evaluates rectangular pulses centered at each delay.
